aliases: get my session id from a bash command
why a child session id differs from the task output path
resolve the live session id for goal feedback
blurb: Debugging or extending how a Bash command recovers its own Claude Code session id.
intent: How a Bash command recovers its own Claude Code session id. Claude Code (>= ~2.1) exports the live id into every child process's environment as `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`; `lib/session_probe.py::resolve()` reads it and the always-present `regin session-id` subcommand prints it (empty stdout + exit 0 on a miss, so callers treat blank as 'omit the flag'). For a child session the env var is the child's own id — where that context's trace spans land — so skills read it here rather than reconstructing it from a Task tool's output path. Consumed by `goal preflight --session-id`, `goal feedback --trace-id`, and the `gate` anti-skip checks.
label: Session-id probe (recovering the live Claude Code session id inside a Bash command)
